operating_state: Net sales decreased to $5.3 billion from $6.6 billion driven by lower crude oil, NGL and natural gas sales volumes partially offset by higher commodity prices. Oil and gas production averaged 1,113 Mboe/d, down 319 Mboe/d year-over-year primarily reflecting Permian and DJ Basin declines from reduced capital investment. Chemical segment benefited from improved pricing while midstream results improved from crude export sales timing.
cost_structure: Oil and gas operating expenses decreased from lower surface operations and maintenance costs, partially offset by winter storm expenses. Transportation costs declined reflecting lower domestic production volumes while purchased commodity costs increased from higher crude prices. DD&A expense decreased from lower production volumes but was partially offset by higher per-barrel rates due to lower proved reserves.
material_events: Occidental repaid $174 million debt upon maturity and divested non-operated DJ Basin assets while selling 11.5 million WES units generating $102 million gain. Gary Hu was elected to the Board as Icahn Group designee replacing Nicholas Graziano. CEO base salary was partially restored to $1.0 million effective January 1, 2021.
macro_exposures: Operations remain highly dependent on oil prices, NGL and natural gas prices, Midland-to-Gulf-Coast spreads and chemical product pricing. Oil price recovery faces headwinds from potential COVID-19 resurgence and slow vaccine distribution in major economies. Near-term pricing influenced by OPEC+ supply decisions and pandemic duration.
forward_outlook: Occidental targets sustaining production in-line with Q4 2020 rate with $2.9 billion annualized capital budget while maintaining majority of 2020 cost savings. Company intends using excess cash flow and asset sale proceeds to strengthen balance sheet by reducing debt and financial obligations. Additional asset sales expected later in 2021.
uncertainty_profile: Zero Coupon senior notes due 2036 can be put to Occidental in October 2021 for approximately $1.0 billion. Interest rate swaps totaling $1.5 billion notional value face mandatory termination through 2023. Environmental compliance costs expected to rise with ongoing remedial activities and a $403 million loss contingency exists from former Ecuador operations.
